sections.paragraph:
plur. שָׁתֹות m. columns (from the root שִׁית), and metaph. princes, nobles. Psa 11:3, “when the columns are overturned,” i.e. when the noblest, the defenders of what is right and good, have perished. Isa 19:10, “and the columns thereof (of Egypt) are broken down,” i.e. the foremost of the state. Opp. to the hired labourers, i.e. the common people.

I. שֵׁת-

(1) buttock, Isa 20:4 plur. שְׁתֹות 2Sa 10:4. Arab. إِسْتُ, Syriac plur. ܐܶܫܬܝܺܢ id. The origin should be sought in the root שִׁית (compare the German Gefäß), although שְׁתֹות with the forms in Arab. and Syr. follows the analogy of verbs לח֞.

(2) [Seth, Sheth], pr.n. of the third [mentioned] son of Adam, Gen 4:25, 26 5:3 , seqq. In the first of these passages it is derived from placing, setting in the stead of another (as if Erfaß).

II. שֵׁת fem. contr. for שֵׁאת ( Lam 3:47) tumult, from the root שָׁאָה. Num 24:17, בְּנֵי שֵׁת “the sons of the tumult of war,” i.e. the tumultuous enemies of Israel. In Jerem. 48:45 (a passage taken from this in Num.) there is instead, בְּנֵי שָׁאֹון.
